scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked). honest pure-C distance 4 (pins+swap stripped). Full-build SHA1 is the real gate.

sessions.lever: blocked-lane triage — 2 more rules+pins-removed full-build variants (distinct-vars, t+c split), both DIFF and FURTHER from target

reviewer.evidence: candidate.c is HEAD's body which CARRIES the cheats (2 register pins + the regfix swap). Preserved as current-state reference, NOT a clean candidate. The pure-C variants tried this pass are in rejected_forms.

remaining_gap.summary: Tail register-rotation. Target: arg0[0]/[1] -> v0($2), arg0[2] -> v1($3) (loaded early, before mask), mask=0xFF0000 -> v0($2), stores in near-source order. Without pins GCC picks v0 for all three loads + reorders the schedule (loads hoisted, stores reordered) — FURTHER from target, not closer. The 2 pins (t=$2, mask=$3) + the `$2<->$3 @42-46` regfix swap reproduce the exact target allocation. Shared infrastructure across ~10 text1b transform-block siblings (func_800611A4, func_80061250, func_8006156C, func_80061658, func_80061710, func_800617C8, func_80061ACC, func_80061EC0, func_80061FAC, this one).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 1 regfix swap (regfix.txt:3118 `$2 <-> $3 @42-46`) PLUS 2 in-body register pins (cheat-asm). All three must go for COMPLETED-C. No pure-C form found that reaches the target tail allocation; variable-splitting makes it WORSE (extra scheduling).

next_hypotheses: CLUSTER approach: solve ONE sibling first whose tail is the simplest instance of this register-rotation, then transfer. func_800611A4 / func_8006156C etc. all share the pattern; a lever that cracks one likely cracks all. Pick the smallest sibling.

Instrumented allocator dump (cc1 -da .greg + ALLOCDBG, register-alloc-deep-dive.md) on the tail: target wants the LAST-loaded value (arg0[2]) in v1 while the mask reuses v0 — determine the liveness shape that yields {arg0[2]=v1, mask=v0} without pins. The crux: arg0[2]'s load must be schedulable to overlap the mask's lui while NOT taking v0.

Source-order tweak: keep arg0[2]'s load textually LAST (as target) but make mask a longer-lived value materialized so GCC keeps it in v0 and spills arg0[2] to v1. The naive distinct-var split instead hoists arg0[2] and reorders stores — find a form that preserves target's near-sequential schedule.

rejected_forms.form: single t for arg0[0]/[1], distinct c for arg0[2], no pins
rejected_forms.reason: DIFF (sha 974f75f1) — tail uses a0 for loads, v1 for arg0[2], v0 for mask, with loads hoisted + stores reordered; FURTHER from target's near-sequential v0/v1 form.

rejected_forms.form: drop register kw / drop kw + inline mask literal
preload arg0[2] into local before stores
